question: Which CSS feature is used to set the background color of an element?
hint: Use the property that fills the element’s background with a color.
explanation: The background-color property sets an element’s background color.

question: Which CSS feature is used to change the text color?
hint: This property controls the foreground color of text.
explanation: The color property changes text color.

question: Which CSS feature is used to add space inside an element, between the content and border?
hint: Think about inner spacing, not outer spacing.
explanation: Padding adds space inside an element.

question: Which CSS feature is used to add space outside an element’s border?
hint: This is the outer spacing around an element.
explanation: Margin adds space outside an element’s border.

question: Which CSS feature is used to round the corners of an element?
hint: It starts with the word 'border'.
explanation: border-radius rounds the corners of an element.

question: Which CSS feature is used to control an element’s transparency?
hint: A value of 0 makes the element fully transparent.
explanation: opacity controls the transparency of an element.

question: Which CSS feature is used to apply a shadow effect to text?
hint: This property specifically targets characters.
explanation: text-shadow applies a shadow effect to text.

question: Which CSS feature is used to apply a shadow effect to an element box?
hint: This affects the element box itself.
explanation: box-shadow applies a shadow effect to the element’s box.

question: Which CSS feature is used to control the size of text?
hint: It begins with 'font'.
explanation: font-size controls the size of text.

question: Which CSS feature is used to control spacing between letters?
hint: It targets individual characters.
explanation: letter-spacing controls spacing between letters.

question: Which CSS feature is used to control spacing between words?
hint: It affects gaps between whole words.
explanation: word-spacing controls spacing between words.

question: Which CSS feature is used to control the height of each line of text?
hint: It affects vertical spacing between lines.
explanation: line-height sets the height of each line box.

question: Which CSS feature is used to make text bold?
hint: Normal and bold are common values.
explanation: font-weight controls text thickness.

question: Which CSS feature is used to make text italic?
hint: One common value is italic.
explanation: font-style can make text italic.

question: Which CSS feature is used to underline text?
hint: It can also create overlines and line-through effects.
explanation: text-decoration is used to underline text.

question: Which CSS feature is used to convert text to uppercase?
hint: This property changes text casing.
explanation: text-transform can convert text to uppercase.

question: Which CSS feature is used to set the font family of text?
hint: It chooses the typeface stack.
explanation: font-family sets which fonts are used.

question: Which CSS feature is used to align inline content horizontally inside a block container?
hint: This is commonly used with left, center, and right.
explanation: text-align aligns inline content horizontally.

question: Which CSS feature is used to indent the first line of text in a block?
hint: It affects only the first line.
explanation: text-indent indents the first line of text.

question: Which CSS feature is used to control how long an animation takes to complete one cycle?
hint: This property expects a time value like 2s.
explanation: animation-duration sets how long one animation cycle lasts.

question: Which CSS feature is used to delay the start of an animation?
hint: It uses a time value before the animation begins.
explanation: animation-delay delays the start of an animation.

question: Which CSS feature is used to specify the timing curve of an animation?
hint: Examples include ease, linear, and ease-in-out.
explanation: animation-timing-function defines the pacing curve of an animation.

question: Which CSS feature is used to determine whether an animation repeats?
hint: It can be a number or infinite.
explanation: animation-iteration-count controls repetition.

question: Which CSS feature is used to pause or runs an animation?
hint: Common values are running and paused.
explanation: animation-play-state pauses or runs an animation.

question: Which CSS feature is used to choose which CSS property should transition?
hint: It names the property that should animate on change.
explanation: transition-property specifies which property transitions.

question: Which CSS feature is used to control how long a transition lasts?
hint: It uses values like 200ms or 1s.
explanation: transition-duration sets the duration of a transition.

question: Which CSS feature is used to delay the start of a transition?
hint: It postpones the transition after a change occurs.
explanation: transition-delay delays the start of a transition.

question: Which CSS feature is used to define the speed curve of a transition?
hint: It controls easing for transitions.
explanation: transition-timing-function defines the pacing curve of a transition.

question: Which CSS feature is used to set an element to use Flexbox layout?
hint: You enable the layout by changing the display mode.
explanation: display: flex makes an element a flex container.

question: Which CSS feature is used to set an element to use CSS Grid layout?
hint: You enable grid by changing the display mode.
explanation: display: grid makes an element a grid container.

question: Which CSS feature is used to change the main axis of a flex container from row to column?
hint: This property controls the main axis direction.
explanation: flex-direction sets the direction of flex items.

question: Which CSS feature is used to distributes flex items along the main axis?
hint: This affects the main axis.
explanation: justify-content aligns flex items along the main axis.

question: Which CSS feature is used to align flex items along the cross axis?
hint: This affects the cross axis for a single line of items.
explanation: align-items aligns flex items along the cross axis.

question: Which CSS feature is used to allow flex items to move onto multiple lines?
hint: Without it, items stay on a single line by default.
explanation: flex-wrap controls whether flex items wrap.

question: Which CSS feature is used to combine flex-direction and flex-wrap into one shorthand?
hint: It combines two flex container properties.
explanation: flex-flow is shorthand for flex-direction and flex-wrap.

question: Which CSS feature is used to control the initial main size of a flex item before remaining space is distributed?
hint: It acts as the starting size in flex layout.
explanation: flex-basis sets the initial main size of a flex item.

question: Which CSS feature is used to allow a flex item to grow and take extra space?
hint: A value greater than 0 lets it expand.
explanation: flex-grow controls how a flex item grows.

question: Which CSS feature is used to control how a flex item shrinks when space is limited?
hint: It works when the container is too small.
explanation: flex-shrink controls how a flex item shrinks.

question: Which CSS feature is used to change the visual order of a flex item without changing the HTML order?
hint: This affects the sequence of flex items.
explanation: order changes the visual order of flex items.

question: Which CSS feature is used to define the columns of a CSS Grid container?
hint: It specifies the track sizes for columns.
explanation: grid-template-columns defines grid columns.

question: Which CSS feature is used to define the rows of a CSS Grid container?
hint: It specifies the track sizes for rows.
explanation: grid-template-rows defines grid rows.

question: Which CSS feature is used to places a grid item along the row tracks?
hint: It is used on the grid item itself.
explanation: grid-row controls a grid item’s row placement.

question: Which CSS feature is used to places a grid item along the column tracks?
hint: It is used on the grid item itself.
explanation: grid-column controls a grid item’s column placement.

question: Which CSS feature is used to set the gap between rows and columns in Grid or Flex layouts?
hint: This property works on modern grid and flex containers.
explanation: gap sets spacing between items in grid and flex layouts.

question: Which CSS feature is used to set only the row gap in a Grid or Flex layout?
hint: It targets vertical spacing between tracks or wrapped lines.
explanation: row-gap sets only row spacing.

question: Which CSS feature is used to set only the column gap in a Grid or Flex layout?
hint: It targets horizontal spacing between tracks or columns.
explanation: column-gap sets only column spacing.

question: Which CSS feature is used to position an element relative to its normal position?
hint: Offsets are applied from the element’s normal position.
explanation: position: relative keeps the element in normal flow and allows offsets.

question: Which CSS feature is used to remove an element from normal flow and positions it relative to the nearest positioned ancestor?
hint: It is often used inside a relatively positioned parent.
explanation: position: absolute positions an element relative to the nearest positioned ancestor.

question: Which CSS feature is used to keep an element fixed relative to the viewport?
hint: It stays in place when the page scrolls.
explanation: position: fixed positions an element relative to the viewport.

question: Which CSS feature is used to behave like relative until a scroll threshold is reached, then sticks?
hint: It is useful for sticky headers.
explanation: position: sticky sticks an element after a threshold is reached.

question: Which CSS feature is used to control stacking order for positioned elements?
hint: Higher values generally appear in front.
explanation: z-index controls stacking order.

question: Which CSS feature is used to clip overflowing content or adds scrollbars?
hint: Common values include hidden, auto, and scroll.
explanation: overflow controls what happens when content exceeds an element’s box.

question: Which CSS feature is used to control only horizontal overflow?
hint: It affects left-to-right overflow behavior.
explanation: overflow-x controls horizontal overflow.

question: Which CSS feature is used to control only vertical overflow?
hint: It affects top-to-bottom overflow behavior.
explanation: overflow-y controls vertical overflow.

question: Which CSS feature is used to change the mouse cursor when hovering an element?
hint: One common value is pointer.
explanation: cursor changes the mouse cursor over an element.

question: Which CSS feature is used to hides an element but still keeps its layout space reserved?
hint: The element is invisible, but its space remains.
explanation: visibility: hidden hides an element while keeping its layout space.

question: Which CSS feature is used to remove an element from the layout entirely?
hint: The element no longer takes up space.
explanation: display: none removes an element from the layout flow.

question: Which CSS feature is used to limit the maximum width of an element?
hint: It prevents the element from growing beyond a certain size.
explanation: max-width sets the maximum width of an element.

question: Which CSS feature is used to ensure an element cannot become narrower than a certain value?
hint: It defines the minimum allowed width.
explanation: min-width sets the minimum width of an element.

question: Which CSS feature is used to limit the maximum height of an element?
hint: It prevents the element from becoming taller than a certain size.
explanation: max-height sets the maximum height of an element.

question: Which CSS feature is used to ensure an element cannot become shorter than a certain value?
hint: It defines the minimum allowed height.
explanation: min-height sets the minimum height of an element.

question: Which CSS feature is used to control how replaced content like an image should fit inside its box?
hint: Common values include cover and contain.
explanation: object-fit controls how replaced content fits inside its box.

question: Which CSS feature is used to set the position of replaced content like an image inside its box?
hint: It works together with object-fit.
explanation: object-position sets the alignment of replaced content inside its box.

question: Which CSS feature is used to set the color of a text cursor in an input or editable area?
hint: It affects the blinking insertion point.
explanation: caret-color sets the color of the text insertion caret.

question: Which CSS feature is used to set the highlight color for certain native form controls in supporting browsers?
hint: It customizes the browser-provided accent color.
explanation: accent-color sets the accent color of some native controls.

question: Which CSS feature is used to change how the browser draws native form control styling?
hint: Setting it to none can remove platform-native styling.
explanation: appearance controls native styling of UI elements.

question: Which CSS feature is used to prevent text from wrapping onto multiple lines?
hint: One common value is nowrap.
explanation: white-space controls how whitespace and line breaks are handled.

question: Which CSS feature is used to allow long words to break and wrap to the next line when necessary?
hint: It used to be known as word-wrap.
explanation: overflow-wrap allows long words to wrap.

question: Which CSS feature is used to control how words break when they reach the end of a line?
hint: It affects line-breaking behavior for words.
explanation: word-break controls how words break at line boundaries.

question: Which CSS feature is used to add hyphenation opportunities to text in supported languages?
hint: Automatic hyphenation depends on language support.
explanation: hyphens controls hyphenation behavior.

question: Which CSS feature is used to show an ellipsis when inline content overflows a single line box?
hint: It is commonly used with nowrap and hidden overflow.
explanation: text-overflow can display an ellipsis for clipped text.

question: Which CSS feature is used to define a grid track size range with a minimum and maximum value?
hint: It is often used in grid-template-columns.
explanation: minmax() defines a size range for a grid track.

question: Which CSS feature is used to repeats a grid track pattern in Grid layouts?
hint: It reduces repetition in grid-template definitions.
explanation: repeat() repeats a grid track pattern.

question: Which CSS feature is used to create a custom property reference in CSS?
hint: It is used with custom properties like --brand-color.
explanation: var() reads the value of a custom property.

question: Which CSS feature is used to perform calculations such as 100% - 2rem in CSS?
hint: It can combine different compatible units.
explanation: calc() performs calculations in CSS.

question: Which CSS feature is used to clamps a responsive value between a minimum and maximum?
hint: It accepts three arguments: min, preferred, and max.
explanation: clamp() constrains a value between minimum and maximum bounds.

question: Which CSS feature is used to load custom fonts defined by the author?
hint: This at-rule defines downloadable fonts.
explanation: @font-face lets authors define custom fonts.

question: Which CSS feature is used to import another style sheet into the current one?
hint: It is an at-rule placed near the top of a stylesheet.
explanation: @import imports another stylesheet.

question: Which CSS feature is used to apply CSS only when a media condition matches?
hint: It is used for responsive design and media queries.
explanation: @media applies rules conditionally based on media features.

question: Which CSS feature is used to apply CSS only if the browser supports a feature?
hint: It is useful for feature queries.
explanation: @supports checks whether the browser supports a CSS feature.

question: Which CSS feature is used to style an element when it is hovered by the pointer?
hint: It is a pseudo-class triggered by pointer hover.
explanation: :hover matches when the pointing device is over an element.

question: Which CSS feature is used to style an element while it is being activated, such as during a mouse click?
hint: This pseudo-class is usually brief during interaction.
explanation: :active matches an element while it is being activated.

question: Which CSS feature is used to style a link that has already been visited?
hint: It applies only to visited hyperlinks.
explanation: :visited matches links the user has already visited.

question: Which CSS feature is used to style an element when it receives keyboard or programmatic focus?
hint: It applies when the element is focused.
explanation: :focus matches a focused element.

question: Which CSS feature is used to style an element when focus should be visibly indicated, usually for keyboard navigation?
hint: It helps avoid unnecessary focus rings for pointer interactions.
explanation: :focus-visible matches focused elements when a visible focus indicator is appropriate.

question: Which CSS feature is used to style a parent element when it or one of its descendants has focus?
hint: This is useful for styling form groups.
explanation: :focus-within matches an element if it or a descendant has focus.

question: Which CSS feature is used to style a checkbox or radio input when it is selected?
hint: It applies to checked form controls.
explanation: :checked matches checked radio buttons and checkboxes.

question: Which CSS feature is used to style a form control that is disabled?
hint: This pseudo-class applies to disabled controls.
explanation: :disabled matches disabled form controls.

question: Which CSS feature is used to style a form control that is enabled?
hint: It is the opposite of :disabled.
explanation: :enabled matches enabled form controls.

question: Which CSS feature is used to match an element when it is the target of the current URL fragment?
hint: It is related to hash links in the URL.
explanation: :target matches the element referenced by the URL fragment.

question: Which CSS feature is used to create generated content before an element’s actual content?
hint: It is a pseudo-element, not a pseudo-class.
explanation: ::before inserts generated content before an element’s content.

question: Which CSS feature is used to create generated content after an element’s actual content?
hint: It is a pseudo-element, not a pseudo-class.
explanation: ::after inserts generated content after an element’s content.

question: Which CSS feature is used to style the selected portion of text?
hint: It affects text selected by the user.
explanation: ::selection styles selected text.

question: Which CSS feature is used to style placeholder text inside an input or textarea?
hint: This pseudo-element targets placeholder text.
explanation: ::placeholder styles placeholder text.

question: Which CSS feature is used to match the direct child of a parent in a selector like nav > a?
hint: This combinator selects only immediate children.
explanation: The > combinator matches direct children.

question: Which CSS feature is used to match the adjacent sibling in a selector like h2 + p?
hint: It selects the next sibling only.
explanation: The + combinator matches the adjacent sibling.

question: Which CSS feature is used to match all following siblings in a selector like h2 ~ p?
hint: It selects later siblings, not just the next one.
explanation: The ~ combinator matches general following siblings.

question: Which CSS feature is used to match descendants in a selector like article p?
hint: This selector uses a space between selectors.
explanation: A space is the descendant combinator.

question: Which CSS feature is used to select an element by its ID in CSS?
hint: An ID selector begins with a hash symbol.
explanation: #id is the syntax for selecting an element by ID.

question: Which CSS feature is used to select an element by its class in CSS?
hint: A class selector begins with a period.
explanation: .class is the syntax for selecting an element by class.

question: Which CSS feature is used to select all elements in CSS?
hint: This is the universal selector.
explanation: * is the universal selector.

question: Which CSS feature is used to targets elements with an attribute like [type="email"]?
hint: Square brackets are used for this kind of selector.
explanation: Square-bracket selectors are attribute selectors.

question: Which CSS feature is used to set how list item markers should appear?
hint: Values include disc, decimal, and none.
explanation: list-style-type controls the appearance of list markers.

question: Which CSS feature is used to position list item markers inside or outside the content flow?
hint: Its common values are inside and outside.
explanation: list-style-position controls the position of list markers.

question: Which CSS feature is used to remove the default bullet or number marker from a list?
hint: A shorthand with the value none is commonly used.
explanation: list-style: none removes list markers.

question: Which CSS feature is used to specify whether table cells share borders or keep them separated?
hint: A common value is collapse.
explanation: border-collapse controls whether table borders collapse.

question: Which CSS feature is used to set the spacing between table cell borders when borders are separate?
hint: It applies when border-collapse is separate.
explanation: border-spacing sets space between table cell borders.

question: Which CSS feature is used to control the algorithm used for laying out table columns?
hint: Common values are auto and fixed.
explanation: table-layout controls the table column layout algorithm.

question: Which CSS feature is used to draw a line around an element without affecting layout space like border can?
hint: It is drawn outside the border edge.
explanation: outline draws a line around an element without taking up space.

question: Which CSS feature is used to set the style of an element’s border, such as solid or dashed?
hint: This property controls dashed, dotted, or solid lines.
explanation: border-style sets the style of the border.

question: Which CSS feature is used to set the width of an element’s border?
hint: It controls thickness, not color or style.
explanation: border-width sets the width of the border.

question: Which CSS feature is used to set the color of an element’s border?
hint: It controls the border’s color specifically.
explanation: border-color sets the color of the border.

question: Which CSS feature is used to draw only the top border of an element?
hint: This shorthand targets one side only.
explanation: border-top sets the top border.

question: Which CSS feature is used to draw only the left border of an element?
hint: This shorthand targets one side only.
explanation: border-left sets the left border.

question: Which CSS feature is used to control whether the background extends under the border, padding, or content box?
hint: Values include border-box, padding-box, and content-box.
explanation: background-clip controls the painting area of the background.

question: Which CSS feature is used to control whether a background image is positioned relative to the border box, padding box, or content box?
hint: It defines the box used as the positioning area.
explanation: background-origin sets the background positioning area.

question: Which CSS feature is used to set the size of a background image?
hint: Common values include cover and contain.
explanation: background-size sets the size of a background image.

question: Which CSS feature is used to position a background image inside an element?
hint: It controls where the image appears inside the box.
explanation: background-position sets the position of a background image.

question: Which CSS feature is used to control whether a background image repeats?
hint: Common values include no-repeat and repeat-x.
explanation: background-repeat controls whether a background image repeats.

question: Which CSS feature is used to control whether a background image scrolls with the page or stays fixed?
hint: One common value is fixed.
explanation: background-attachment controls scrolling behavior of a background image.
